workflow_rationale: Implementing a new scanner manager component for MT90 barcode scanner integration. This is a greenfield feature requiring Android project setup, core manager implementation, and device testing.

phases.name: Android Project Setup
phases.description: Create Android project structure with Gradle, Hilt dependency injection, and basic Application class

patterns_from: Standard Android Gradle setup from Android Developer documentation
subtasks.notes: Created root Gradle build configuration with build.gradle.kts, settings.gradle.kts, gradle.properties, and gradle wrapper. Verified with ./gradlew --version - Gradle 8.2 working successfully.

subtasks.description: Create app module with Hilt dependencies
subtasks.notes: Created app/build.gradle.kts with Hilt 2.48 dependencies (hilt-android, hilt-android-compiler, hilt-navigation-compose). Configured Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.0, Kotlin 1.9.20, Jetpack Compose, and Coroutines. Created app/proguard-rules.pro with Hilt-specific ProGuard rules. Configured Gradle to use Android Studio JBR (Java 21) via gradle.properties. Verified with ./gradlew app:dependencies - Hilt dependencies properly resolved.

subtasks.description: Create AndroidManifest and Application class with Hilt
subtasks.notes: Created AndroidManifest.xml with required permissions (CAMERA, VIBRATE) and InventoryApplication.kt with @HiltAndroidApp annotation. Added all required resource files (strings, themes, launcher icons, backup rules). Fixed Java 21 compatibility by upgrading Kotlin to 1.9.22, AGP to 8.3.0, Gradle to 8.4, and Compose Compiler to 1.5.8. Configured gradle.properties with in-process Kotlin compilation for KAPT compatibility. Verified with ./gradlew assembleDebug - build successful, APK generated (7.8MB).

phases.name: Scanner Data Models
phases.description: Create ScanResult data class and scanner-related models

subtasks.description: Create ScanResult data class with barcode and barcodeType
subtasks.notes: Created ScanResult.kt data class with barcode and barcodeType properties. Added KDoc comments following project patterns. Updated .gitignore to exclude Android build directories. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful.

phases.name: NewlandScannerManager Implementation
phases.description: Implement the core NewlandScannerManager with BroadcastReceiver, lifecycle management, debouncing, and SharedFlow

subtasks.notes: Created NewlandScannerManager.kt skeleton with @Singleton annotation and @Inject constructor. Injected ApplicationContext for future BroadcastReceiver registration. Added comprehensive KDoc comments following project patterns. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful.

Implemented BroadcastReceiver for nlscan.action.SCANNER_RESULT. Added inner BroadcastReceiver class that handles scan events, extracts barcode data and type from intent extras, and validates inputs. Follows Newland MT90 SDK patterns. Compilation verified successful.

subtasks.description: Add register() and unregister() lifecycle methods
subtasks.notes: Implemented register() and unregister() lifecycle methods for BroadcastReceiver management. Added isRegistered tracking flag to prevent double registration/unregistration. Both methods include comprehensive KDoc comments and logging. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful. Committed changes to git.

subtasks.description: Implement SharedFlow<ScanResult> for reactive event emission
subtasks.notes: Implemented SharedFlow<ScanResult> for reactive event emission. Added CoroutineScope with SupervisorJob + Dispatchers.Main.immediate for safe event emission. Created MutableSharedFlow (replay=0, extraBufferCapacity=1) for internal emission and exposed public SharedFlow via asSharedFlow(). Updated BroadcastReceiver to emit scan results to SharedFlow. Added comprehensive KDoc comments with usage examples. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful. Committed changes to git.

Implemented 300ms debounce protection using timestamp-based comparison. Added DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS constant (300L), lastScanTimestamp tracking variable, and debounce logic in BroadcastReceiver's onReceive method. Scans within 300ms of the previous scan are ignored and logged. Timestamp updated before emitting to prevent race conditions. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful. Committed changes to git.

Implemented hardware button handler for KEYCODE_F6. Added ACTION_SCANNER_TRIG constant, onKeyDown() method to handle F6 key presses, and private triggerScan() method to send scan trigger broadcasts. Includes comprehensive KDoc comments with usage examples for Activities. Verified with ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin - compilation successful. Committed changes to git.
Made triggerScan() method public to enable programmatic triggering from ViewModels and UI components. Added comprehensive documentation with usage examples. Code compiles successfully.

phases.name: Dependency Injection Setup
phases.description: Create Hilt module to provide NewlandScannerManager as singleton

subtasks.description: Create ScannerModule with @Provides annotation for NewlandScannerManager

verification.expected: Build successful with Hilt DI configured

subtasks.notes: Created ScannerModule.kt with @Module, @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class), and @Provides annotations for NewlandScannerManager. The module provides the scanner manager singleton with ApplicationContext injection. Verified with ./gradlew assembleDebug - build successful, Hilt DI properly configured. Committed changes to git.

phases.name: Unit Testing
phases.description: Create unit tests for NewlandScannerManager covering debounce, lifecycle, and SharedFlow emission

subtasks.description: Create test class with debounce verification
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive unit tests for NewlandScannerManager. Added mockito-kotlin and kotlin-test dependencies to build.gradle.kts. Configured testOptions to return default values for Android framework methods. Implemented 13 passing tests covering BroadcastReceiver lifecycle, hardware button handling, programmatic triggering, and input validation. Documented limitation with Dispatchers.Main.immediate testing and provided recommendations for future improvements. Verified with ./gradlew test - all tests pass successfully.

Lifecycle tests already exist from subtask-5-1. The test file contains 4 comprehensive lifecycle tests: (1) register registers BroadcastReceiver with correct filter, (2) register multiple calls do not double-register, (3) unregister unregisters BroadcastReceiver, (4) unregister safe to call when not registered. Verified with ./gradlew test - all tests pass successfully. No additional tests needed.

subtasks.description: Add tests for SharedFlow event emission

phases.name: Device Integration Verification
phases.description: Manual verification on physical MT90 device to ensure scanner works correctly and no memory leaks

subtasks.description: Create sample Activity to test scanner integration

verification.instructions: Deploy to MT90 device and verify: (1) Hardware F6 button triggers scan, (2) Programmatic trigger works, (3) Debounce prevents double-scans, (4) SharedFlow emits events to Activity, (5) Unregister prevents memory leak, (6) No crashes or ANRs

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution required due to phase dependencies

acceptance_criteria: Hardware scan button (F6) works on MT90
No memory leaks - BroadcastReceiver properly unregistered
No crashes or ANRs on device

verification_steps.name: Device Testing
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All acceptance criteria verified on physical device

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk due to lifecycle management concerns (memory leaks), device-specific hardware integration, and concurrency (debouncing). Requires unit tests and physical device validation.

checks: Hardware scan button (F6) triggers barcode scan
BroadcastReceiver unregisters on destroy (no memory leak)
No crashes or ANRs during normal operation
